description_long: This is a rustic log cabin with a prominent gable roof covered in reddish-brown clay tiles, extending well beyond the walls to provide ample overhang. The structure is built from horizontally stacked logs, giving it a traditional and sturdy appearance. The front facade features a double wooden door flanked by a pair of small, square windows with wooden frames, arranged symmetrically. The cabin sits on a flat, green base, suggesting a grassy or landscaped setting. Decorative wooden beams and supports are visible under the roof eaves, adding to the log cabin aesthetic. The rear and side walls are solid log construction with no visible openings, emphasizing privacy and insulation.
description: A traditional log cabin with a wide, tiled gable roof, double front doors, and two small front windows, all set on a green base. The structure features prominent log walls and deep roof overhangs.
description_view_0: The side view highlights the deep overhang of the clay-tiled gable roof and the solid log wall with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_1: The front view shows the double wooden doors centered between two small, square windows, all beneath the wide gable roof.
description_view_2: The opposite side view again emphasizes the roof's overhang and the uninterrupted log wall, with the tops of the front windows just visible.
description_view_3: The rear view displays a solid log wall with no windows or doors, and the gable roof's ridge running along the top.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with deep overhangs and clay tile covering
attribute_window_arrangement: Two small, square windows symmetrically placed on the front facade, flanking the central door; no windows visible on other sides
attribute_door_placement: One double door centered on the front facade, directly beneath the gable
